meaning: I can come by / I can stop by
communicative_purpose: To offer to visit someone or stop by a place
trigger_situations: Offering to visit, suggesting meetups, making casual visits
contexts: Friend visits, dropping by, informal invitations, favors
variations: je peux passer, je peux passer voir, je passera,i je peux passer chez
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'je peux passer' meaning 'I can pass/accept'; timing confusion
collocates: ce soir, demain, après le bureau, quand tu veux
example_1: Je peux passer vers 18h si ça te dit.
example_2: Je peux passer voir comment tu vas si tu veux.
example_3: T'as besoin de quoi ? Je peux passer au supermarché.
example_1_translation: I can come by around 6 p.m. if you'd like.
example_2_translation: I can come see how you're doing if you want.
example_3_translation: What do you need? I can go to the supermarket.

meaning: it's open / I'm open to it
communicative_purpose: To express that something is possible or that one is open to suggestions
trigger_situations: Being flexible, accepting suggestions, open schedules
contexts: Planning, scheduling, being accommodating, casual negotiations
variations: c'est open, je suis open, c'est open bar, open à
common_mistakes: English loanword may sound too casual; mixing with French 'ouvert'
collocates: à tout, aux propositions, à suggestions, flexible
example_1: Pour les vacances, c'est open, tu choisis la destination.
example_2: Je suis open pour changer de restaurant ce soir.
example_3: C'est open cette semaine, pas de plans fixes.
example_1_translation: For holidays, it's open, you choose the destination.
example_2_translation: I'm open to changing restaurants tonight.
example_3_translation: It's open this week, no fixed plans.

meaning: I let myself go / I went all out
communicative_purpose: To admit to having done something excessively or without restraint
trigger_situations: Overspending, overeating, excessive celebration, wild behavior
contexts: Shopping sprees, parties, food indulgences, budget confessions
variations: je me suis lâché, je me suis bien lâché, je me lâche, je lâche rien
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'se lâcher' meaning to relax; reflexive construction errors
example_1: J'ai fait les soldes et je me suis bien lâché sur les achats.
example_2: Au mariage, je me suis lâché sur la dance floor.
example_3: Niveau bouffe, je me suis vraiment lâché hier.
example_1_translation: I went to the sales and indulged in the purchases.
example_2_translation: At the wedding, I let loose on the dance floor.
example_3_translation: Food-wise, I really indulged myself yesterday.

communicative_purpose: To propose an assumption or simplified version of reality
trigger_situations: Making approximations, hypothetical discussions, softening statements
variations: on va dire que, disons que, admettons que, mettons que
common_mistakes: Confusing tone - can sound dismissive or very serious depending on context
collocates: pour, que, genre, environ, à peu près
example_1: On va dire que tu as raison pour simplifier.
example_2: Disons que ça coûte environ 50 euros, ça te va ?
example_3: On va dire que c'est terminé, on passe à autre chose.
example_1_translation: We will say that you are right to simplify.
example_2_translation: Let's say it costs around 50 euros, is that okay with you?
example_3_translation: We'll say it's over, we'll move on.

meaning: I'm busy / I'm loaded / I'm full
communicative_purpose: To indicate being very busy, full up, or having had enough
trigger_situations: No availability, being booked, fullness of stomach or schedule
contexts: Schedules, event capacity, post-meal, busy periods
variations: je suis full, je suis full booked, je suis complet, je suis blindé
common_mistakes: English loanword; confusing with 'je suis plein' (grammatical but less common)
collocates: d'emploi du temps, de travail, de monde, max
example_1: Désolé je suis full cette semaine, on reprogramme ?
example_2: Le restaurant est full ce soir, faut réserver.
example_3: J'ai trop mangé, je suis full comme un ballon.
example_1_translation: Sorry I'm full this week, shall we reschedule?
example_2_translation: The restaurant is full this evening, reservations are required.
example_3_translation: I ate too much, I'm as full as a balloon.

communicative_purpose: To express that something is enjoyable or fun
trigger_situations: Enjoying activities, approving of things, expressing pleasure
contexts: Movies, parties, experiences, social events, food
variations: c'est kiffant, c'est trop kiffant, c'est vraiment kiff, c'est cool
common_mistakes: Overusing 'kiffant'; considering it too slang for formal contexts
example_1: Ce bar est trop kiffant, la déco est incroyable.
example_2: Le concert était kiffant, on a passé une super soirée.
example_3: C'est kiffant de voyager avec toi.
example_1_translation: This bar is so cool, the decor is incredible.
example_2_translation: The concert was great, we had a great evening.
example_3_translation: It's great to travel with you.

meaning: I'm maxed out / I'm ready to go
communicative_purpose: To express being at full capacity or ready with full energy
trigger_situations: Maximum effort, full commitment, or being completely tired
contexts: Sports, work projects, parties, pre-game rituals
variations: je suis à bloc, je suis à fond, je suis à block, je suis au max
common_mistakes: Confusing context - can mean ready or exhausted; clarifying needed
example_1: Je suis à bloc pour le match de demain !
example_2: Cette semaine de rush, je suis à bloc total.
example_3: J'ai rien mangé, je suis à bloc mais crevé.
example_2_translation: This week of rush, I'm at full capacity.
example_3_translation: I haven't eaten anything, I'm full but exhausted.

meaning: where's the problem / what's the issue
communicative_purpose: To ask what the difficulty or issue is
trigger_situations: Troubleshooting, asking for problems, offering help
contexts: Tech support, project work, conflict resolution, friendly help
variations: il est où le problème, c'est où le problème, quel est le problème, où ça coince
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'quel est le problème' (more formal); tone can sound impatient
example_1: J'arrive pas à démarrer, il est où le problème ?
example_2: C'est là que ça marche pas, il est où le problème exactement ?
example_3: Tu comprends rien, où ça coince ? Il est où le problème ?
example_1_translation: I can't start, what's the problem?
example_2_translation: This is where it doesn't work, where exactly is the problem?
example_3_translation: You don't understand, where is it going? Where is the problem?

primary_function: Expression of great pleasure or satisfaction
communicative_purpose: To express that something is fantastic, enjoyable, or makes you happy
trigger_situations: Enjoying life, great experiences, happy moments
contexts: Vacations, fun activities, success, relationships, celebrations
variations: c'est le pied, c'est trop le pied, c'est vraiment le pied, c'est divinement bon
common_mistakes: Confusing with literal foot; 'c'est le pied' is the correct positive expression
example_1: Cette playlist me rappelle ma jeunesse, c'est le pied !
example_2: Les vacances en Grèce, c'était le pied total.
example_3: Quand le patron dit OK, c'est le pied !
example_1_translation: This playlist reminds me of my youth, it’s great!
example_2_translation: The vacation in Greece was a blast.
example_3_translation: When the boss says OK, it’s great!

communicative_purpose: To express nervousness, anxiety, or worry about something
trigger_situations: Before exams, important events, stressful situations, fear
contexts: Test anxiety, job interviews, social situations, scary movies
variations: je flippe, je flippe grave, je flip à mort, je stresse
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'flipper' (to flip/switch); using it too casually for serious anxiety
example_1: J'ai mon entretien demain et je flippe grave !
example_2: Je flippe que le vol soit annulé à cause de la grève.
example_3: Trop de monde me stresses, je flippe dans ces cas-là.
example_1_translation: I have my interview tomorrow and I'm really freaking out!
example_2_translation: I'm worried that the flight will be canceled because of the strike.
example_3_translation: Too many people stress me out, I freak out in these cases.

communicative_purpose: To indicate that a deadline has passed or opportunity is lost
trigger_situations: Missing deadlines, lost opportunities, time passed
contexts: Work deadlines, apologies, regrets, missed chances
variations: c'est trop tard, c'est trop tard, il est trop tard, on a raté
common_mistakes: English mixing in formal contexts; overusing when more specific expressions exist
collocates: pour, maintenant, rien à faire, malheureusement
example_1: C'est too late pour changer les résultats maintenant.
example_2: Trop tard pour s'excuser, elle est partie.
example_3: Le match a commencé, c'est trop tard pour rejoindre.
example_1_translation: It's too late to change the results now.
example_2_translation: Too late to apologize, she left.
example_3_translation: The match has started, it's too late to join.

meaning: I don't care / I couldn't care less
communicative_purpose: To express complete lack of interest or concern
trigger_situations: Showing indifference, dismissing topics, not caring about outcomes
contexts: Gossip, drama, opinions, social situations, arguments
variations: je m'en bats, je m'en fiche, je m'en moque, ça m'est égal, bof
common_mistakes: This is a vulgar expression (literally crude); using it in polite company
collocates: royalement, complétement, total, pas du tout
example_1: Qu'ils disent ce qu'ils veulent, je m'en bats !
example_2: Le résultat des élections, je m'en fiche royalement.
example_3: Ce drama de celebrities, je m'en bats complétement.
example_1_translation: They say what they want, I don't care!
example_2_translation: I don't really care about the election results.
example_3_translation: I don't really care about this celebrity drama.

communicative_purpose: To express embarrassment, shame, or that something is discreditable
trigger_situations: Embarrassing situations, failed attempts, social blunders, scandals
contexts: Failed stories, awkward moments, scandals, embarrassments
variations: c'est la honte, quelle honte, c'est vraiment la honte, c'est une honte
common_mistakes: Confusing 'honte' (shame) with other emotions; literal translation issues
example_1: J'ai mélangé les noms devant tout le monde, c'était la honte.
example_2: Quel honte ce que j'ai fait hier soir, j'ose plus sortir.
example_3: Le scandale politique, c'est la honte pour le pays.
example_1_translation: I mixed up the names in front of everyone, it was a shame.
example_2_translation: What a shame what I did last night, I don't dare go out anymore.
example_3_translation: The political scandal is shame for the country.

meaning: I've got this / I handle it
communicative_purpose: To express confidence in handling a situation
trigger_situations: Showing competence, taking charge, reassuring others
contexts: Work, projects, social situations, crisis management
variations: je gère, je gère tout, je gère grave, ça gaze
common_mistakes: Sounding arrogant in some contexts; overusing it
example_1: Pas de souci pour la présentation, je gère !
example_2: Le dîner ce soir ? Je gère tout, compte sur moi.
example_3: Cette équipe est HS mais je gère la situation.
example_1_translation: No worries about the presentation, I manage!
example_2_translation: Dinner tonight? I manage everything, count on me.
example_3_translation: This team is HS but I manage the situation.

meaning: did you like it / did you enjoy it
communicative_purpose: To ask if someone enjoyed an experience or event
trigger_situations: After events, sharing experiences, checking on enjoyment
contexts: Concerts, parties, dates, meals, trips, movies
variations: t'as kiffé, t'as aimé, c'était bien, t'as passé un bon moment
common_mistakes: Missing the past participle agreement; using too casual for formal contexts
collocates: le concert, la soirée, ce restau, ce film
example_1: T'as kiffé la fête hier ? C'était trop bien !
example_2: Le sushi, t'as kiffé ? C'était mon Idea du coup.
example_3: Vous avez kiffé les vacances au Portugal ?
example_1_translation: Did you enjoy the party yesterday? It was so good!
example_2_translation: Did you like sushi? It was my Idea at the time.
example_3_translation: Did you enjoy your holidays in Portugal?
